revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{皇室祭祀}}神宮奏事始は、日本の伝統的な儀式であり、毎年正月の4日<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁 |url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html |website=www.kunaicho.go.jp |access-date=2024-04-15}}</ref>に行われます。この儀式では、天皇が新年を迎えるにあたり、伊勢神宮に関する政務を視察し、新年の幕開けを祝います…」
revision.text:
{{皇室祭祀}}神宮奏事始は、日本の伝統的な儀式であり、毎年正月の4日<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁 |url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html |website=www.kunaicho.go.jp |access-date=2024-04-15}}</ref>に行われます。この儀式では、天皇が新年を迎えるにあたり、伊勢神宮に関する政務を視察し、新年の幕開けを祝います。神宮伝奏と呼ばれる儀式が行われ、神宮の建設や神職の任命、官位などが報告され、天皇から承認される形で進められます。
奏事始は1月4日に執り行われ、掌典長が伊勢神宮や宮中の祭事について天皇に報告する年始の重要な儀式です。この行事は新年の計画と祭典の進捗状況を確認し、古来から続く伝統を象徴しています。<ref name=":0" />
== 歴史 == 神宮奏事始は室町時代に定着した行事です。明治時代まで太政官政府によって継続されました。この儀式は日本の皇室と神道の伝統から生まれ、国家の平和と繁栄を願う重要な役割を果たしてきました。<ref name=":0" />
== 意義 == 神宮奏事始は伊勢神宮への敬意表明であり、国家と人々の安全を願うと共に天皇と神社間の結びつきを象徴するものです。また伊勢神宮の地位や皇族崇拝でも特別な位置づけがあります。<ref name=":0" />
== 脚注 == <references /> [[Category:皇室の儀式]]

奏事始は1月4日に執り行われ、掌典長が伊勢神宮や宮中の祭事について天皇に報告する年始の重要な儀式です。この行事は新年の計画と祭典の進捗状況を確認し、古来から続く伝統を象徴しています。<ref name=":1" />
== 歴史 == 神宮奏事始は室町時代に定着した行事です。明治時代まで太政官政府によって継続されました。この儀式は日本の皇室と神道の伝統から生まれ、国家の平和と繁栄を願う重要な役割を果たしてきました。<ref name=":1" />

== 脚注 == <references /> [[Category:皇室の儀式]]

== 意義 == 神宮奏事始は伊勢神宮への敬意表明であり、国家と人々の安全を願うと共に天皇と神社間の結びつきを象徴するものである。また伊勢神宮の地位や皇族崇拝でも特別な位置づけがある<ref name=":1" />。

revision.text:
{{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''Start of Musical Performance'''|神宮奏事始|Jingū sōjihajime}} is a traditional [[Japan|Japanese]] ceremony. It is held every year on the 4th day of the New Year <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> . During this ceremony, [[Emperor of Japan|the Emperor]] inspects government affairs related to Ise Shrine and celebrates the start of the new year. A ceremony called Jingu Densō is held, during which the construction of the shrine, the appointment of priests, and official ranks are reported and then approved by the emperor. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典|first=精選版|title=神宮奏事始(じんぐうそうじはじめ)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%AE%E5%A5%8F%E4%BA%8B%E5%A7%8B-2051907|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
The Sojihajime ceremony is held on January 4th, when the Grand Master of Ceremonies reports to the Emperor about the festivals being held at Ise Shrine and the Imperial Palace. The event checks the progress of new year plans and celebrations and symbolizes ancient traditions. <ref name=":1" />
== History == The Jingu Sojihajime is an event that became established during the Muromachi period. It was continued by the Daijokan government until the Meiji period. The ceremony originated in Japan's imperial and Shinto traditions and has played an important role in praying for peace and prosperity for the nation. <ref name=":1" />
== Significance == The Jingu Sojihajime ceremony is an expression of respect to Ise Shrine, a prayer for the safety of the nation and its people, and symbolizes the bond between the Emperor and the shrine. It also has a special place in the status of Ise Shrine and in imperial worship. <ref name=":1" />
== References ==

During this ceremony, [[Emperor of Japan|the Emperor]] inspects government affairs related to Ise Shrine and celebrates the start of the new year. A ceremony called Jingu Densō is held, during which the construction of the shrine, the appointment of priests, and official ranks are reported and then approved by the emperor. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典|first=精選版|title=神宮奏事始(じんぐうそうじはじめ)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%AE%E5%A5%8F%E4%BA%8B%E5%A7%8B-2051907|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
The Sojihajime ceremony is held on January 4th, when the Grand Master of Ceremonies reports to the Emperor about the festivals being held at Ise Shrine and the Imperial Palace. The event checks the progress of new year plans and celebrations and symbolizes ancient traditions. <ref name=":1" />
== History == The Jingu Sojihajime is an event that became established during the Muromachi period. It was continued by the Daijokan government until the Meiji period. The ceremony originated in Japan's imperial and Shinto traditions and has played an important role in praying for peace and prosperity for the nation. <ref name=":1" />
== Significance == The Jingu Sojihajime ceremony is an expression of respect to Ise Shrine, a prayer for the safety of the nation and its people, and symbolizes the bond between the Emperor and the shrine. It also has a special place in the status of Ise Shrine and in imperial worship. <ref name=":1" />
== References ==

The Sojihajime ceremony is held on January 4th, when the Grand Master of Ceremonies reports to the Emperor about the festivals being held at Ise Shrine and the Imperial Palace. The event checks the progress of new year plans and celebrations and symbolizes ancient traditions. <ref name=":1" />
== History == The Jingu Sojihajime is an event that became established during the Muromachi period. It was continued by the Daijokan government until the Meiji period. The ceremony originated in Japan's imperial and Shinto traditions and has played an important role in praying for peace and prosperity for the nation. <ref name=":1" />
== Significance == The Jingu Sojihajime ceremony is an expression of respect to Ise Shrine, a prayer for the safety of the nation and its people, and symbolizes the bond between the Emperor and the shrine. It also has a special place in the status of Ise Shrine and in imperial worship. <ref name=":1" />
== References ==

The Sojihajime ceremony is held on January 4th, when the Grand Master of Ceremonies reports to the Emperor about the festivals being held at Ise Shrine and the Imperial Palace. The event checks the progress of new year plans and celebrations and symbolizes ancient traditions. <ref name=":1" />
== History == The Jingu Sojihajime is an event that became established during the Muromachi period. It was continued by the Daijokan government until the Meiji period. The ceremony originated in Japan's imperial and Shinto traditions and has played an important role in praying for peace and prosperity for the nation. <ref name=":1" />
== Significance == The Jingu Sojihajime ceremony is an expression of respect to Ise Shrine, a prayer for the safety of the nation and its people, and symbolizes the bond between the Emperor and the shrine. It also has a special place in the status of Ise Shrine and in imperial worship. <ref name=":1" />

{{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''Start of Musical Performance'''|神宮奏事始|Jingū sōjihajime}} is a traditional [[Japan|Japanese]] ceremony. It is held every year on the 4th day of the New Year <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> . During this ceremony, [[Emperor of Japan|the Emperor]] inspects government affairs related to Ise Shrine and celebrates the start of the new year. A ceremony called Jingu Densō is held, during which the construction of the shrine, the appointment of priests, and official ranks are reported and then approved by the emperor. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典|first=精選版|title=神宮奏事始(じんぐうそうじはじめ)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%AE%E5%A5%8F%E4%BA%8B%E5%A7%8B-2051907|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
The Sojihajime ceremony is held on January 4th, when the Grand Master of Ceremonies reports to the Emperor about the festivals being held at Ise Shrine and the Imperial Palace. The event checks the progress of new year plans and celebrations and symbolizes ancient traditions. <ref name=":1" />
== History == The Jingu Sojihajime is an event that became established during the Muromachi period. It was continued by the Daijokan government until the Meiji period. The ceremony originated in Japan's imperial and Shinto traditions and has played an important role in praying for peace and prosperity for the nation. <ref name=":1" />
== Significance == The Jingu Sojihajime ceremony is an expression of respect to Ise Shrine, a prayer for the safety of the nation and its people, and symbolizes the bond between the Emperor and the shrine. It also has a special place in the status of Ise Shrine and in imperial worship. <ref name=":1" />
== References ==
[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sōji Hajime]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sōji Hajime]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sōji Hajime]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sōji Hajime]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Start of Musical Performance]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sōji Hajime]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Start of Musical Performance]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Start of Musical Performance]][[ja:奏事始]] [[en:Start of Musical Performance]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[en:Start of Musical Performance]]<references group="lower-alpha"/>

revision.comment: スタイル修正、読み仮名付与。
revision.text:
{{皇室祭祀}} '''奏事始'''（そうじはじめ）は、[[日本]]の伝統的な儀式。毎年正月の4日<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁 |url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html |website=www.kunaicho.go.jp |access-date=2024-04-15}}</ref>に行われる。この儀式では、[[天皇]]が新年を迎えるにあたり、伊勢神宮に関する政務を視察し、新年の幕開けを祝う。神宮伝奏と呼ばれる儀式が行われ、神宮の建設や神職の任命、官位などが報告され、天皇から承認される形で進められる<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=神宮奏事始(じんぐうそうじはじめ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%AE%E5%A5%8F%E4%BA%8B%E5%A7%8B-2051907 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-04-15 |language=ja |first=精選版 |last=日本国語大辞典}}</ref>。
奏事始は1月4日に執り行われ、掌典長が伊勢神宮や宮中の祭事について天皇に報告する。この行事は新年の計画と祭典の進捗状況を確認し、古来から続く伝統を象徴する<ref name=":1" />。
== 歴史 == 神宮奏事始は室町時代に定着した行事である。明治時代まで太政官政府によって継続された。この儀式は日本の皇室と神道の伝統から生まれ、国家の平和と繁栄を願う重要な役割を果たしてきた<ref name=":1" />。
== 意義 == 神宮奏事始は伊勢神宮への敬意表明であり、国家と人々の安全を願うと共に天皇と神社間の結びつきを象徴するものである。また伊勢神宮の地位や皇族崇拝でも特別な位置づけがある<ref name=":1" />。
== 脚注 == <references />{{デフォルトソート:そうしはしめ}} [[Category:皇室の儀式]]

A ceremony called Jingu Densō is held, during which the construction of the shrine, the appointment of priests, and official ranks are reported and then approved by the emperor. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典|first=精選版|title=神宮奏事始(じんぐうそうじはじめ)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%AE%E5%A5%8F%E4%BA%8B%E5%A7%8B-2051907|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
The Sojihajime ceremony is held on January 4th, when the Grand Master of Ceremonies reports to the Emperor about the festivals being held at Ise Shrine and the Imperial Palace. The event checks the progress of new year plans and celebrations and symbolizes ancient traditions. <ref name=":1" />
== History == The Jingu Sojihajime is an event that became established during the Muromachi period. It was continued by the Daijokan government until the Meiji period. The ceremony originated in Japan's imperial and Shinto traditions and has played an important role in praying for peace and prosperity for the nation. <ref name=":1" />
== Significance == The Jingu Sojihajime ceremony is an expression of respect to Ise Shrine, a prayer for the safety of the nation and its people, and symbolizes the bond between the Emperor and the shrine. It also has a special place in the status of Ise Shrine and in imperial worship. <ref name=":1" />
== References ==

It also has a special place in the status of Ise Shrine and in imperial worship. <ref name=":1" />

{{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''Start of Musical Performance'''|神宮奏事始|Jingū sōjihajime}} is a traditional [[Japan|Japanese]] ceremony. It is held every year on the 4th day of the New Year <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> . During this ceremony, [[Emperor of Japan|the Emperor]] inspects government affairs related to Ise Shrine and celebrates the start of the new year. A ceremony called Jingu Densō is held, during which the construction of the shrine, the appointment of priests, and official ranks are reported and then approved by the emperor. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典|first=精選版|title=神宮奏事始(じんぐうそうじはじめ)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%AE%E5%A5%8F%E4%BA%8B%E5%A7%8B-2051907|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
The Sojihajime ceremony is held on January 4th, when the Grand Master of Ceremonies reports to the Emperor about the festivals being held at Ise Shrine and the Imperial Palace. The event checks the progress of new year plans and celebrations and symbolizes ancient traditions. <ref name=":1" />
== History == The Jingu Sojihajime is an event that became established during the Muromachi period. It was continued by the Daijokan government until the Meiji period. The ceremony originated in Japan's imperial and Shinto traditions and has played an important role in praying for peace and prosperity for the nation. <ref name=":1" />
== Significance == The Jingu Sojihajime ceremony is an expression of respect to Ise Shrine, a prayer for the safety of the nation and its people, and symbolizes the bond between the Emperor and the shrine. It also has a special place in the status of Ise Shrine and in imperial worship. <ref name=":1" />

A ceremony called Jingu Densō is held, during which the construction of the shrine, the appointment of priests, and official ranks are reported and then approved by the emperor. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典|first=精選版|title=神宮奏事始(じんぐうそうじはじめ)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%AE%E5%A5%8F%E4%BA%8B%E5%A7%8B-2051907|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
The Sojihajime ceremony is held on January 4th, when the Grand Master of Ceremonies reports to the Emperor about the festivals being held at Ise Shrine and the Imperial Palace. The event checks the progress of new year plans and celebrations and symbolizes ancient traditions. <ref name=":1" />
== History == The Jingu Sojihajime is an event that became established during the Muromachi period. It was continued by the Daijokan government until the Meiji period. The ceremony originated in Japan's imperial and Shinto traditions and has played an important role in praying for peace and prosperity for the nation. <ref name=":1" />
== Significance == The Jingu Sojihajime ceremony is an expression of respect to Ise Shrine, a prayer for the safety of the nation and its people, and symbolizes the bond between the Emperor and the shrine. It also has a special place in the status of Ise Shrine and in imperial worship. <ref name=":1" />

revision.text:
{{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''Start of Musical Performance'''|神宮奏事始|Jingū sōjihajime}} is a traditional [[Japan|Japanese]] ceremony. It is held every year on the 4th day of the New Year <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> . During this ceremony, [[Emperor of Japan|the Emperor]] inspects government affairs related to Ise Shrine and celebrates the start of the new year. A ceremony called Jingu Densō is held, during which the construction of the shrine, the appointment of priests, and official ranks are reported and then approved by the emperor. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典|first=精選版|title=神宮奏事始(じんぐうそうじはじめ)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%AE%E5%A5%8F%E4%BA%8B%E5%A7%8B-2051907|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
The Sojihajime ceremony is held on January 4th, when the Grand Master of Ceremonies reports to the Emperor about the festivals being held at Ise Shrine and the Imperial Palace. The event checks the progress of new year plans and celebrations and symbolizes ancient traditions. <ref name=":1" />
== History == The Jingu Sojihajime is an event that became established during the Muromachi period. It was continued by the Daijokan government until the Meiji period. The ceremony originated in Japan's imperial and Shinto traditions and has played an important role in praying for peace and prosperity for the nation. <ref name=":1" />
== Significance == The Jingu Sojihajime ceremony is an expression of respect to Ise Shrine, a prayer for the safety of the nation and its people, and symbolizes the bond between the Emperor and the shrine. It also has a special place in the status of Ise Shrine and in imperial worship. <ref name=":1" />
== References ==

A ceremony called Jingu Densō is held, during which the construction of the shrine, the appointment of priests, and official ranks are reported and then approved by the emperor. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典|first=精選版|title=神宮奏事始(じんぐうそうじはじめ)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%AE%E5%A5%8F%E4%BA%8B%E5%A7%8B-2051907|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
The Sojihajime ceremony is held on January 4th, when the Grand Master of Ceremonies reports to the Emperor about the festivals being held at Ise Shrine and the Imperial Palace. The event checks the progress of new year plans and celebrations and symbolizes ancient traditions. <ref name=":1" />
== History == The Jingu Sojihajime is an event that became established during the Muromachi period. It was continued by the Daijokan government until the Meiji period. The ceremony originated in Japan's imperial and Shinto traditions and has played an important role in praying for peace and prosperity for the nation. <ref name=":1" />
== Significance == The Jingu Sojihajime ceremony is an expression of respect to Ise Shrine, a prayer for the safety of the nation and its people, and symbolizes the bond between the Emperor and the shrine. It also has a special place in the status of Ise Shrine and in imperial worship. <ref name=":1" />

revision.text:
{{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''Start of Musical Performance'''|神宮奏事始|Jingū sōjihajime}} is a traditional [[Japan|Japanese]] ceremony. It is held every year on the 4th day of the New Year <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> . During this ceremony, [[Emperor of Japan|the Emperor]] inspects government affairs related to Ise Shrine and celebrates the start of the new year. A ceremony called Jingu Densō is held, during which the construction of the shrine, the appointment of priests, and official ranks are reported and then approved by the emperor. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典|first=精選版|title=神宮奏事始(じんぐうそうじはじめ)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%AE%E5%A5%8F%E4%BA%8B%E5%A7%8B-2051907|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
The Sojihajime ceremony is held on January 4th, when the Grand Master of Ceremonies reports to the Emperor about the festivals being held at Ise Shrine and the Imperial Palace. The event checks the progress of new year plans and celebrations and symbolizes ancient traditions. <ref name=":1" />
== History == The Jingu Sojihajime is an event that became established during the Muromachi period. It was continued by the Daijokan government until the Meiji period. The ceremony originated in Japan's imperial and Shinto traditions and has played an important role in praying for peace and prosperity for the nation. <ref name=":1" />
== Significance == The Jingu Sojihajime ceremony is an expression of respect to Ise Shrine, a prayer for the safety of the nation and its people, and symbolizes the bond between the Emperor and the shrine. It also has a special place in the status of Ise Shrine and in imperial worship. <ref name=":1" />
== References ==

revision.text:
{{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''Start of Musical Performance'''|神宮奏事始|Jingū sōjihajime}} is a traditional [[Japan|Japanese]] ceremony. It is held every year on the 4th day of the New Year <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> . During this ceremony, [[Emperor of Japan|the Emperor]] inspects government affairs related to Ise Shrine and celebrates the start of the new year. A ceremony called Jingu Densō is held, during which the construction of the shrine, the appointment of priests, and official ranks are reported and then approved by the emperor. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典|first=精選版|title=神宮奏事始(じんぐうそうじはじめ)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%AE%E5%A5%8F%E4%BA%8B%E5%A7%8B-2051907|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
The Sojihajime ceremony is held on January 4th, when the Grand Master of Ceremonies reports to the Emperor about the festivals being held at Ise Shrine and the Imperial Palace. The event checks the progress of new year plans and celebrations and symbolizes ancient traditions. <ref name=":1" />
== History == The Jingu Sojihajime is an event that became established during the Muromachi period. It was continued by the Daijokan government until the Meiji period. The ceremony originated in Japan's imperial and Shinto traditions and has played an important role in praying for peace and prosperity for the nation. <ref name=":1" />
== Significance == The Jingu Sojihajime ceremony is an expression of respect to Ise Shrine, a prayer for the safety of the nation and its people, and symbolizes the bond between the Emperor and the shrine. It also has a special place in the status of Ise Shrine and in imperial worship. <ref name=":1" />
== References ==

revision.text:
{{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''Start of Musical Performance'''|神宮奏事始|Jingū sōjihajime}} is a traditional [[Japan|Japanese]] ceremony. It is held every year on the 4th day of the New Year <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> . During this ceremony, [[Emperor of Japan|the Emperor]] inspects government affairs related to Ise Shrine and celebrates the start of the new year. A ceremony called Jingu Densō is held, during which the construction of the shrine, the appointment of priests, and official ranks are reported and then approved by the emperor. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典|first=精選版|title=神宮奏事始(じんぐうそうじはじめ)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%AE%E5%A5%8F%E4%BA%8B%E5%A7%8B-2051907|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
The Sojihajime ceremony is held on January 4th, when the Grand Master of Ceremonies reports to the Emperor about the festivals being held at Ise Shrine and the Imperial Palace. The event checks the progress of new year plans and celebrations and symbolizes ancient traditions. <ref name=":1" />
== History == The Jingu Sojihajime is an event that became established during the Muromachi period. It was continued by the Daijokan government until the Meiji period. The ceremony originated in Japan's imperial and Shinto traditions and has played an important role in praying for peace and prosperity for the nation. <ref name=":1" />
== Significance == The Jingu Sojihajime ceremony is an expression of respect to Ise Shrine, a prayer for the safety of the nation and its people, and symbolizes the bond between the Emperor and the shrine. It also has a special place in the status of Ise Shrine and in imperial worship. <ref name=":1" />
== References ==

A ceremony called Jingu Densō is held, during which the construction of the shrine, the appointment of priests, and official ranks are reported and then approved by the emperor. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典|first=精選版|title=神宮奏事始(じんぐうそうじはじめ)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%AE%E5%A5%8F%E4%BA%8B%E5%A7%8B-2051907|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
The Sojihajime ceremony is held on January 4th, when the Grand Master of Ceremonies reports to the Emperor about the festivals being held at Ise Shrine and the Imperial Palace. The event checks the progress of new year plans and celebrations and symbolizes ancient traditions. <ref name=":1" />
== History == The Jingu Sojihajime is an event that became established during the Muromachi period. It was continued by the Daijokan government until the Meiji period. The ceremony originated in Japan's imperial and Shinto traditions and has played an important role in praying for peace and prosperity for the nation. <ref name=":1" />
== Significance == The Jingu Sojihajime ceremony is an expression of respect to Ise Shrine, a prayer for the safety of the nation and its people, and symbolizes the bond between the Emperor and the shrine. It also has a special place in the status of Ise Shrine and in imperial worship. <ref name=":1" />

revision.text:
{{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''Start of Musical Performance'''|神宮奏事始|Jingū sōjihajime}} is a traditional [[Japan|Japanese]] ceremony. It is held every year on the 4th day of the New Year <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> . During this ceremony, [[Emperor of Japan|the Emperor]] inspects government affairs related to Ise Shrine and celebrates the start of the new year. A ceremony called Jingu Densō is held, during which the construction of the shrine, the appointment of priests, and official ranks are reported and then approved by the emperor. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典|first=精選版|title=神宮奏事始(じんぐうそうじはじめ)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%AE%E5%A5%8F%E4%BA%8B%E5%A7%8B-2051907|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
The Sojihajime ceremony is held on January 4th, when the Grand Master of Ceremonies reports to the Emperor about the festivals being held at Ise Shrine and the Imperial Palace. The event checks the progress of new year plans and celebrations and symbolizes ancient traditions. <ref name=":1" />
== History == The Jingu Sojihajime is an event that became established during the Muromachi period. It was continued by the Daijokan government until the Meiji period. The ceremony originated in Japan's imperial and Shinto traditions and has played an important role in praying for peace and prosperity for the nation. <ref name=":1" />
== Significance == The Jingu Sojihajime ceremony is an expression of respect to Ise Shrine, a prayer for the safety of the nation and its people, and symbolizes the bond between the Emperor and the shrine. It also has a special place in the status of Ise Shrine and in imperial worship. <ref name=":1" />
== References == <!--Sōji Hajime-->
<!--Sōji Hajime-->
<!--Sōji Hajime-->
<!--Sōji Hajime-->[[simple:user:immanuelle/Start of Musical Performance]]
<!--Sōji Hajime--> [[simple:user:immanuelle/Start of Musical Performance]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Start of Musical Performance]]

revision.text:
{{皇室祭祀}} '''奏事始'''（そうじはじめ）は、[[日本]]の伝統的な儀式。毎年正月の4日<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁 |url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html |website=www.kunaicho.go.jp |access-date=2024-04-15}}</ref>に行われる。この儀式では、[[天皇]]が新年を迎えるにあたり、伊勢神宮に関する政務を視察し、新年の幕開けを祝う。神宮伝奏と呼ばれる儀式が行われ、神宮の建設や神職の任命、官位などが報告され、天皇から承認される形で進められる<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=神宮奏事始(じんぐうそうじはじめ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%AE%E5%A5%8F%E4%BA%8B%E5%A7%8B-2051907 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-04-15 |language=ja |first=精選版 |last=日本国語大辞典}}</ref>。
奏事始は1月4日に執り行われ、掌典長が伊勢神宮や宮中の祭事について天皇に報告する。この行事は新年の計画と祭典の進捗状況を確認し、古来から続く伝統を象徴する<ref name=":1" />。
== 歴史 == 神宮奏事始は室町時代に定着した行事である。明治時代まで太政官政府によって継続された。この儀式は日本の皇室と神道の伝統から生まれ、国家の平和と繁栄を願う重要な役割を果たしてきた<ref name=":1" />。
== 意義 == 神宮奏事始は伊勢神宮への敬意表明であり、国家と人々の安全を願うと共に天皇と神社間の結びつきを象徴するものである。また伊勢神宮の地位や皇族崇拝でも特別な位置づけがある<ref name=":1" />。
== 脚注 == <references />{{デフォルトソート:そうしはしめ}} [[Category:皇室の儀式]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[奏事始]] to [[Sōji Hajime]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''Start of Musical Performance'''|神宮奏事始|Jingū sōjihajime}} is a traditional [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] ceremony. It is held every year on the 4th day of the New Year <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> . During this ceremony, [[:en:Emperor of Japan|the Emperor]] inspects government affairs related to Ise Shrine and celebrates the start of the new year. A ceremony called Jingu Densō is held, during which the construction of the shrine, the appointment of priests, and official Ranks are reported and then approved by the emperor. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典|first=精選版|title=神宮奏事始(じんぐうそうじはじめ)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%AE%E5%A5%8F%E4%BA%8B%E5%A7%8B-2051907|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
The Sojihajime ceremony is held on January 4th, when the Grand Master of Ceremonies reports to the Emperor about the festivals being held at Ise Shrine and the Imperial Palace. The event checks the progress of new year plans and celebrations and symbolizes ancient traditions. <ref name=":1" />
== History == The Jingu Sojihajime is an event that became established during the Muromachi period. It was continued by the Daijokan government until the Meiji period. The ceremony originated in Japan's imperial and Shinto traditions and has played an important role in praying for peace and prosperity for the nation. <ref name=":1" />
== Significance == The Jingu Sojihajime ceremony is an expression of respect to Ise Shrine, a prayer for the safety of the nation and its people, and symbolizes the bond between the Emperor and the shrine. It also has a special place in the status of Ise Shrine and in imperial worship. <ref name=":1" />
== References ==
<references group="lower-alpha"/>
{{translated page|ja|奏事始|version=103698995|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]]
}}

The Sojihajime ceremony is held on January 4th, when the Grand Master of Ceremonies reports to the Emperor about the festivals being held at Ise Shrine and the Imperial Palace. The event checks the progress of new year plans and celebrations and symbolizes ancient traditions. <ref name=":1" />
== History == The Jingu Sojihajime is an event that became established during the Muromachi period. It was continued by the Daijokan government until the Meiji period. The ceremony originated in Japan's imperial and Shinto traditions and has played an important role in praying for peace and prosperity for the nation. <ref name=":1" />
== Significance == The Jingu Sojihajime ceremony is an expression of respect to Ise Shrine, a prayer for the safety of the nation and its people, and symbolizes the bond between the Emperor and the shrine. It also has a special place in the status of Ise Shrine and in imperial worship. <ref name=":1" />

[[sn:ōji Hajime]]
<!--Sōji Hajime--><!--Sōji Hajime--><!--Sōji Hajime--><!--Sōji Hajime--><!--Sōji Hajime--> [[Category:Japanese Imperial Rituals]] [[Category:pages with former wikipedia userpage links]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove incorrect Shona (sn:) interwiki links
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''Start of Musical Performance'''|神宮奏事始|Jingū sōjihajime}} is a traditional [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] ceremony. It is held every year on the 4th day of the New Year <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> . During this ceremony, [[:en:Emperor of Japan|the Emperor]] inspects government affairs related to Ise Shrine and celebrates the start of the new year. A ceremony called Jingu Densō is held, during which the construction of the shrine, the appointment of priests, and official Ranks are reported and then approved by the emperor. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典|first=精選版|title=神宮奏事始(じんぐうそうじはじめ)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%AE%E5%A5%8F%E4%BA%8B%E5%A7%8B-2051907|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
The Sojihajime ceremony is held on January 4th, when the Grand Master of Ceremonies reports to the Emperor about the festivals being held at Ise Shrine and the Imperial Palace. The event checks the progress of new year plans and celebrations and symbolizes ancient traditions. <ref name=":1" />
== History == The Jingu Sojihajime is an event that became established during the Muromachi period. It was continued by the Daijokan government until the Meiji period. The ceremony originated in Japan's imperial and Shinto traditions and has played an important role in praying for peace and prosperity for the nation. <ref name=":1" />
== Significance == The Jingu Sojihajime ceremony is an expression of respect to Ise Shrine, a prayer for the safety of the nation and its people, and symbolizes the bond between the Emperor and the shrine. It also has a special place in the status of Ise Shrine and in imperial worship. <ref name=":1" />
== References ==

The Sojihajime ceremony is held on January 4th, when the Grand Master of Ceremonies reports to the Emperor about the festivals being held at Ise Shrine and the Imperial Palace. The event checks the progress of new year plans and celebrations and symbolizes ancient traditions. <ref name=":1" />
== History == The Jingu Sojihajime is an event that became established during the Muromachi period. It was continued by the Daijokan government until the Meiji period. The ceremony originated in Japan's imperial and Shinto traditions and has played an important role in praying for peace and prosperity for the nation. <ref name=":1" />
== Significance == The Jingu Sojihajime ceremony is an expression of respect to Ise Shrine, a prayer for the safety of the nation and its people, and symbolizes the bond between the Emperor and the shrine. It also has a special place in the status of Ise Shrine and in imperial worship. <ref name=":1" />

<!--Sōji Hajime--><!--Sōji Hajime--><!--Sōji Hajime--><!--Sōji Hajime--><!--Sōji Hajime--> [[Category:Japanese Imperial Rituals]] [[en:Sōji Hajime]] {{wikidata link|Q136731786}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:奏事始]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''Start of Musical Performance'''|神宮奏事始|Jingū sōjihajime}} is a traditional [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] ceremony. It is held every year on the 4th day of the New Year <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> . During this ceremony, [[:en:Emperor of Japan|the Emperor]] inspects government affairs related to Ise Shrine and celebrates the start of the new year. A ceremony called Jingu Densō is held, during which the construction of the shrine, the appointment of priests, and official Ranks are reported and then approved by the emperor. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典|first=精選版|title=神宮奏事始(じんぐうそうじはじめ)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%AE%E5%A5%8F%E4%BA%8B%E5%A7%8B-2051907|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
The Sojihajime ceremony is held on January 4th, when the Grand Master of Ceremonies reports to the Emperor about the festivals being held at Ise Shrine and the Imperial Palace. The event checks the progress of new year plans and celebrations and symbolizes ancient traditions. <ref name=":1" />
== History == The Jingu Sojihajime is an event that became established during the Muromachi period. It was continued by the Daijokan government until the Meiji period. The ceremony originated in Japan's imperial and Shinto traditions and has played an important role in praying for peace and prosperity for the nation. <ref name=":1" />
== Significance == The Jingu Sojihajime ceremony is an expression of respect to Ise Shrine, a prayer for the safety of the nation and its people, and symbolizes the bond between the Emperor and the shrine. It also has a special place in the status of Ise Shrine and in imperial worship. <ref name=":1" />
== References ==

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:奏事始]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:奏事始]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:奏事始]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}}
'''The''' {{Nihongo|'''Start of Musical Performance'''|神宮奏事始|Jingū sōjihajime}} is a traditional {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japanese}} ceremony. It is held every year on the 4th day of the New Year <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=主要祭儀一覧 - 宮内庁|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/about/gokomu/kyuchu/saishi/saishi01.html|access-date=2024-04-15|website=www.kunaicho.go.jp}}</ref> . During this ceremony, {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=the Emperor}} inspects government affairs related to Ise Shrine and celebrates the start of the new year. A ceremony called Jingu Densō is held, during which the construction of the shrine, the appointment of priests, and official Ranks are reported and then approved by the emperor. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典|first=精選版|title=神宮奏事始(じんぐうそうじはじめ)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%AE%E5%A5%8F%E4%BA%8B%E5%A7%8B-2051907|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
The Sojihajime ceremony is held on January 4th, when the Grand Master of Ceremonies reports to the Emperor about the festivals being held at Ise Shrine and the Imperial Palace. The event checks the progress of new year plans and celebrations and symbolizes ancient traditions. <ref name=":1" />
== History == The Jingu Sojihajime is an event that became established during the Muromachi period. It was continued by the Daijokan government until the Meiji period. The ceremony originated in Japan's imperial and Shinto traditions and has played an important role in praying for peace and prosperity for the nation. <ref name=":1" />
== Significance == The Jingu Sojihajime ceremony is an expression of respect to Ise Shrine, a prayer for the safety of the nation and its people, and symbolizes the bond between the Emperor and the shrine. It also has a special place in the status of Ise Shrine and in imperial worship. <ref name=":1" />
== References ==
<references group="lower-alpha"/>
{{translated page|ja|奏事始|version=103698995|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:奏事始]]

A ceremony called Jingu Densō is held, during which the construction of the shrine, the appointment of priests, and official Ranks are reported and then approved by the emperor. <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|last=日本国語大辞典|first=精選版|title=神宮奏事始(じんぐうそうじはじめ)とは？ 意味や使い方|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%AE%E5%A5%8F%E4%BA%8B%E5%A7%8B-2051907|access-date=2024-04-15|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
The Sojihajime ceremony is held on January 4th, when the Grand Master of Ceremonies reports to the Emperor about the festivals being held at Ise Shrine and the Imperial Palace. The event checks the progress of new year plans and celebrations and symbolizes ancient traditions. <ref name=":1" />
== History == The Jingu Sojihajime is an event that became established during the Muromachi period. It was continued by the Daijokan government until the Meiji period. The ceremony originated in Japan's imperial and Shinto traditions and has played an important role in praying for peace and prosperity for the nation. <ref name=":1" />
== Significance == The Jingu Sojihajime ceremony is an expression of respect to Ise Shrine, a prayer for the safety of the nation and its people, and symbolizes the bond between the Emperor and the shrine. It also has a special place in the status of Ise Shrine and in imperial worship. <ref name=":1" />
